title: paspalum effusum
intro: Paspalum effusum, also known as Bahamagrass, is a type of perennial grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Paspalum effusum include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and treating various infections. In traditional herbal preparations, Paspalum effusum is often used in infusions or decoctions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
